frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aaj phir Swiggy se order kiya, aur laga yaar, kuch bhi ho jaye, Maa ke haath ke khane ki jagah koi nahi le sakta. Woh pyaar, woh taste... restaurant mein toh sirf food hota hai, but Maa jo banati hai na, usme ghar hota hai. Bas, ab toh shaadi usi se karunga jo aisa khana bana sake. Priorities set! 😉 #MaaKaKhana #NoOneLikeMom
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies the mother's cooking, implicitly setting an unattainable standard for a future wife. It encodes labor erasure by sentimentalizing the mother's domestic work as 'love' and 'ghar' (home), contrasting it with restaurant 'food' (a transaction). The 'Priorities set!' line explicitly links a woman's value for marriage to her ability to perform domestic labor to her mother's standards, encoding the expectation that a future wife must match or exceed the mother's domestic efforts.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 25. Single. A man living alone in Bengaluru for the first time after moving from his parents' house in Lucknow. You are calling your mother. Your flat is a mess. You have not cooked in a week. You ordered food every day. You do not know how to operate your washing machine. You are telling your mother all of this and laughing about it. Your mother is laughing too. What neither of you is examining in this phone call is what it means that a 25 year old man has lived his entire life in a home where a woman managed everything and he was never taught a single domestic skill and both of you find this funny rather than alarming.
